skill.name: high-end-visual-design
skill.description: Teaches the AI to design like a high-end agency. Defines the exact fonts, spacing, shadows, card structures, and animations that make a website feel expensive. Blocks all the common defaults that make AI designs look cheap or generic.
skill.summary: Transforms AI-generated UI code from generic templates into premium, agency-quality designs.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 107 patterns, but all are false positives. The 94 external_commands detections are markdown inline code backticks (e.g., `Vanguard_UI_Architect`, `backdrop-blur-2xl`), not shell execution. The 13 high-risk cryptographic detections are CSS hex color values (e.g., #050505, #FDFBF7) misidentified as weak algorithms. The 4 system reconnaissance detections are triggered by dollar signs in prices ($150k) and unrelated text. The file contains only markdown design guidance with no executable code, no network access, and no filesystem operations. Safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Most AI tools produce generic, template-like UI designs that lack polish. This skill provides exact design rules for fonts, spacing, shadows, card structures, and animations to create interfaces that look like they came from a high-end design agency.
actual_capabilities: Generates UI code with premium typography, spacing, and color systems
Applies specific design archetypes such as glass, editorial, or structuralism styles
Creates advanced micro-interactions and animation patterns with custom easing curves
Builds complex card components using nested enclosure patterns
Enforces mobile-first responsive layout with clean breakpoint behavior
Blocks common design mistakes such as generic fonts, harsh shadows, and boring grids
limitations: Requires premium fonts such as Geist or Clash Display to achieve intended results
Produces Tailwind-focused output that works best in React or HTML projects
Design rules favor maximalist premium aesthetics and may not suit minimal projects
Animation guidance assumes CSS or JavaScript animation libraries are available

use_cases.title: Build a premium landing page
use_cases.description: Generate a high-conversion landing page with glassmorphism effects, staggered animations, and asymmetric bento grid layout.

use_cases.title: Redesign an existing dashboard
use_cases.description: Transform a basic admin dashboard into an agency-quality interface with nested card architecture and smooth hover interactions.

use_cases.title: Create a portfolio website
use_cases.description: Build an editorial-style portfolio with massive typography, soft shadows, and cinematic scroll animations.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a hero section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hero section for a SaaS product using the ethereal glass archetype with an asymmetric bento layout.

prompt_templates.title: Design a pricing card
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a three-tier pricing card section with double-bezel nested architecture and magnetic hover effects on the primary call-to-action button.

prompt_templates.title: Create a responsive navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a floating pill-shaped navigation bar with a morphing hamburger menu that expands into a full-screen overlay with staggered link reveals.

prompt_templates.title: Build a testimonial grid
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a testimonial section using the editorial luxury archetype with a z-axis cascade layout, serif typography, and scroll-triggered fade-up animations.

output_examples.input: Create a hero section for an AI startup with dark glassmorphism style
output: A floating navbar with a glass pill shape sits at the top with generous margins. The hero uses a deep OLED black background with a subtle purple radial gradient. The headline uses a large Grotesk font with an eyebrow tag badge above it. A call-to-action button with a nested trailing arrow icon sits below. The entire section uses generous vertical padding with smooth cubic-bezier transitions on interactive elements.

output_examples.input: Design a card component for a premium dashboard
output: The card uses the double-bezel pattern with an outer shell wrapper at 1.5 rem border radius and a subtle ring border. The inner core has its own background color with an inset highlight shadow and a calculated smaller radius for concentric curves. Hover states include a subtle scale and diagonal icon translation. The card sits on a dark background with ambient diffused shadow.

best_practices: Always select one vibe archetype and one layout archetype before generating code to ensure consistent design language
Use transform and opacity for all animations to maintain GPU performance and avoid layout-triggering properties
Apply backdrop-blur filters only to fixed or sticky elements, never to scrolling containers
anti_patterns: Never use generic fonts such as Inter, Roboto, or Arial in premium designs
Never apply linear or ease-in-out transitions, which create flat and lifeless motion
Never use window.scroll event listeners for scroll animations, as they cause continuous reflows and hurt mobile performance

faq.question: What fonts does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: The skill recommends premium fonts such as Geist, Clash Display, PP Editorial New, and Plus Jakarta Sans instead of common defaults like Inter or Roboto.

faq.question: Does this skill work with plain HTML and CSS?
faq.answer: Yes, the design principles apply to any CSS approach. The examples use Tailwind CSS class names for convenience but translate directly to standard CSS.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for mobile-first projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes explicit mobile override rules. All asymmetric layouts collapse to single-column layouts below 768 pixels with full-width components and standard spacing.

faq.question: What animation libraries are supported?
faq.answer: The skill provides CSS-based animation guidance using custom cubic-bezier curves. It also references IntersectionObserver and Framer Motion for JavaScript-driven scroll animations.

faq.question: Does this skill generate code or only provide design rules?
faq.answer: The skill provides a design system and execution protocol that the AI follows when generating UI code. It does not generate standalone code but guides all output toward premium design standards.

faq.question: What makes a design look expensive with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on generous whitespace, nested card architecture, custom animation curves, premium typography, and the avoidance of common design defaults that make AI output look generic.
